consumed.role: mi per degree longitude at equator
tripwire criterion 2: battery min Spearman rho
tripwire criterion 1: min demand-coef bootstrap positive-sign fraction

consumed.value: three-way demand-block fit (pooled all 6 FYs / pre-2020-only fy2017+fy2019 / full-panel with a post2020 x {l_flows, l_zveh_hh} interaction); DOWNGRADE the pooled criterion-1 pass to reported-only (ordinal_ok forced false, decision_format threshold_shortlist, regime_split_downgrade flag) IFF the pooled demand block PASSES criterion 1 but the pre-2020-only demand block does NOT independently pass it -- same screen_pos_frac_min = 0.841 bar, each demand coefficient; no new threshold

bootstrap.acs_moe_note: the §3.4 within-replicate ACS-MOE perturbation is NOT applied: the committed §9.2 block-apportionment input machinery (screen_common_v21, frozen phase-2a under its no-fit hold) carries no ACS MOE, and the design-stage power check pre-registered this omission as a stated stylization (screen_power_check.json). An input-availability fact, not a tuning choice; it removes one noise source, so criterion 1 is marginally MORE lenient (stated)
bootstrap.method: route-cluster resample with replacement, refit, rescore all windows jointly (spec 01 §3.4)

diagnostics.note: analytic cluster-robust min |t| over the demand block is a DIAGNOSTIC (cluster SEs downward-biased at few clusters). b4 (l_genjobs) sits OUTSIDE the demand block; its per-replicate sign fraction and the b4_wrong_sign flag are diagnostics (§9.1)

decision_output.note: decision tripwire v2 on the v2.1 rebuild (spec 01 §5/§9.5). ordinal_ok requires criterion 1 (each demand coef b1/b2 bootstrap positive-sign fraction >= screen_pos_frac_min), criterion 2 (battery min Spearman rho >= screen_battery_rho_min over all 20 frozen rows), criterion 3 (DUAL: window-unit AND host-shape tie-churn both <= their caps), AND no §9.10 regime_split_downgrade. While ordinal_ok is false the gate-1 memo consumes the threshold shortlist plus measured indicators, never a top-N by rank; if shortlist_stability shows heavy churn the honest output is the NARROWER stable core (spec 01 §4b)

regime_split.note: §9.10 pre-registered gate (screen_regime_split): pooled / pre-2020-only (fy2017+fy2019) / full-panel-with-post2020-interaction. BINDING DOWNGRADE: pooled PASSES criterion 1 but pre-2020 does NOT independently pass the SAME screen_pos_frac_min bar -> ordinal_ok forced false, decision_format threshold_shortlist. Reuses the exact criterion-1 statistic; no new threshold

replicate_signs.note: per-replicate signs from the headline route-cluster bootstrap ('+' iff strictly positive; length n_boot); pos_frac recomputes from these

disclaimer: ordinal screening index; not a ridership forecast (spec 00 §1)

fit_diagnostics.b3_label: allocation control -- not a service response (spec 02 owns that question)

b4_wrong_sign.note: §9.1 pre-registered handling: a negative l_genjobs does NOT trip the demand-block criterion (b4 is outside the block) but raises this logged diagnostic -- a signal that b3/b5 may be absorbing attraction effects; setting it OBLIGATES a README known-issue entry before any gate memo consumes the artifact

fit_diagnostics.estimator: log-OLS on log(annual boardings), cluster-robust SEs by route; NB2 always fitted (nb_estimator row)
fit_diagnostics.fit_side_shapes: contemporaneous archived GTFS per boardings year (§9.4); scan side on current 2026-07 GTFS
fit_diagnostics.grouped_decomposition_note: per-window decomposition is GROUPED (demand b1+b2 / generator b4) vs the lower-median fitted host route's best window; demand coefficients are individually weak so per-coefficient attribution is noise attribution (spec 01 §3.1)
service_levels: standardized (svc_std x length; all published scores)
incumbent-actual (diagnostic: LOO errors + underservice gap only)

notes.fit_scan_asymmetry: fit side uses contemporaneous archived GTFS shapes per boardings year (§9.4, discontinued routes re-enter); scan side on current 2026-07 GTFS (candidates must be buildable today)
notes.index: screen_index = 100 * exp(window pred at standardized service - baseline); baseline = lower-median over fitted host routes of each route's own BEST window pred (same-exposure rebase; ordinal only)
notes.sensitivity_pct: pct = 100 * (1 - Spearman rho of the full window ranking vs headline)
notes.tie_with_cutoff: 95% joint bootstrap rank interval straddles the rank-5 boundary (gate-1 tie rule)
notes.zero_handling: b1/b2/b4 consume catchment sums through log1p

overlap_diagnostics.note: connected-component grouping (§3.3) is degenerate at county scale; gate 1 uses best_window_per_shape + per-pair shares below (no transitive closure)

detail.note: best-window-per-host-shape over 40 shapes eligible in both scans; churn ids are host shapes
best-window-per-host-shape over 34 shapes eligible in both scans; churn ids are host shapes

sensitivity.label: b2 = B08141 E002 zero-veh workers (v2.0 headline)
drop one NAICS sector from the CNS15-18 b4 sum (worst of CNS15/CNS16/CNS17/CNS18)

detail.note: single additive b3 term shifts every window identically; index invariant by construction
single additive b3 term shifts every window identically; index invariant by construction
grouping only -- scores/ranking unchanged by construction
grouping only -- scores/ranking unchanged by construction
restored to the v2.1 battery (§9.3/§9.8) because vintage-matched X varies within route across years; leave-one-year-out over the extended 6-FY panel

sensitivity.label: leave-one-year-out rank stability (min rho over the 6-FY panel year-drops)

shortlist_stability.note: per frozen v2.1 battery row (screen_battery_rows_v21): the row's OWN route-cluster bootstrap tie_with_cutoff set vs the MARGIN-DEFINED headline tie set. CRITERION 3 = DUAL THRESHOLD: max_tie_churn_frac_window over the WINDOW-UNIT rows (cap screen_tie_churn_max_window) and max_tie_churn_frac_hostshape over window_10/window_15 (cap screen_tie_churn_max_hostshape); both must pass. min_jaccard is an all-rows report. NO ACS-MOE perturbation (see the bootstrap note); per-row CRN seed rule (default_rng(seed) per row). stable_core = headline tie windows in the tie set under EVERY row (host-shape membership for the window-length rows; every NAICS sector for genjobs_leave_class_out; the worst-year-dropped model for loyo)

per_row.note: tie-set from the worst-year-dropped model (fy fy2019); loyo's published rho is the min over the 6 year-drops

universe.scan_rule: all current-GTFS weekday route shapes with main-shape length >= window length; no functional-class filter
